frameworks.name: signal-detection theory (meta-d')
frameworks.one_line: Metacognitive efficiency measured separately for 2AFC versus yes/no judgments.

frameworks.name: domain-general vs domain-specific metacognition
frameworks.one_line: Tests whether a shared metacognitive faculty spans perception and memory.

core_question: Does the apparent domain-generality of metacognition depend on whether perception and memory tasks share the same judgment format (2AFC vs YN)?
key_assumptions: Metacognitive efficiency is comparable across tasks only when judgment format is matched.
Conflicting prior findings reflect task-format differences rather than true mechanism differences.
manipulations: Judgment format pairing: 2AFC/2AFC (mirroring McCurdy 2013) vs 2AFC/YN (mirroring Baird 2013) vs YN/YN.
Domain pairing: visual perception task with memory task.
key_findings: 2AFC/2AFC replication produced a positive perception-memory metacognitive efficiency correlation.
2AFC/YN replication failed to produce the correlation.
YN/YN pairing also failed to produce a perception-memory metacognitive correlation.
interpretations: Cross-domain metacognitive correlations may be an artifact of shared 2AFC format rather than evidence for a domain-general mechanism.
